verdict_reasoning: SRC is canonically an N-myristoylated non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane with no TM domain or GPI anchor. However, a 2026 Science study (Zhou et al.) demonstrated that in cancer cells, SRC undergoes topological inversion via autophagolysosomal exocytosis (ALE), appearing as extracellular membrane-associated eSrc on the outer leaflet. This was confirmed in cancer cell lines, xenografts, and primary patient tumors. Anti-Src antibody therapeutics (ADC, bispecific TCE, radioligand) targeting eSrc kill tumor cells in vitro and in vivo. This cancer-state ecto-pool qualifies as cell_state_induced surface display restricted to oncogenic transformation.
